title: Door Lock Error
shortDescription: The door lock mechanism has failed to engage.
fullDescription: The E1 error code on IFB washers indicates the door lock assembly cannot secure the door. IFB is a leading Indian appliance brand, and E1 is one of the most common error codes on their front-loading washers.

causes.cause: Wiring fault between lock and board

symptoms: Washer won't start and displays E1

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door seal and latch area for clothing, debris, or small items preventing full closure.

fixSteps.title: Verify alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Close door slowly, observe latch. Tighten hinge screws if misaligned.

fixSteps.title: Test door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug. Access door lock. Test continuity with multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Swap old lock for new. Reconnect wiring.

whenToCallPro: If control board suspected. IFB has a strong service network in India.
videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E1 error door lock fix

faq.q: How to unlock door with E1?
faq.a: Unplug for 3 minutes. If still locked, check for emergency release behind the drain filter panel.

faq.q: Can I get IFB service at home?
faq.a: Yes. IFB offers doorstep service across India through their service network.

faq.q: Are IFB door locks interchangeable between models?
faq.a: No. Each model series uses a specific lock. Use the correct part number for your model.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer cannot drain water within allowed time.
fullDescription: E2 indicates the washer failed to drain within approximately 8 minutes. Common in IFB front loaders when the drain filter is clogged with lint or small items.

fixSteps.title: Clean drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the small door at the bottom front of the washer. Place towels and a pan. Slowly unscrew the filter and clean thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for kinks and blockages. Ensure not inserted too deep into standpipe.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With filter out, check for objects blocking the impeller using a flashlight.

fixSteps.title: Test drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug and test pump motor resistance (5-20 ohms normal).

videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E2 error drain fix

faq.a: Use the drain filter at the bottom front of the machine.
Yes. IFB recommends using their own detergent or HE-compatible detergent.
Monthly, or after every 15-20 washes.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The washer did not fill with water in the expected time.
fullDescription: E3 means insufficient water level within approximately 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure water supply tap is fully open.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect inlet hose. Clean the mesh filter screen.

fixSteps.title: Check water pressure
fixSteps.instruction: IFB washers need minimum 0.3 to 10 bar pressure. If using overhead tank, ensure adequate height.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Test solenoid resistance (500-1500 ohms).

videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E3 error water fill fix

faq.a: IFB recommends minimum 0.3 bar pressure. An overhead tank at adequate height usually provides sufficient pressure.

faq.q: Can I use a water pump?
faq.a: Yes. A small booster pump can help if water pressure is low.
Water pressure often drops during summer months in India due to supply issues.

title: Overflow Error
fullDescription: E4 indicates water above safe threshold, triggering overflow protection.

fixSteps.title: Shut off water
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply tap immediately.

fixSteps.title: Check pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove top panel. Verify the small rubber hose to pressure switch is connected and not kinked.

fixSteps.title: Test pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Blow into port. Listen for click.

fixSteps.title: Check inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With washer unplugged, check if water drips through. Replace if stuck.

whenToCallPro: If inlet valve stuck open. Flood risk.
videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E4 error overflow fix

faq.a: Yes. Turn off water immediately.

faq.q: Can a clogged pressure hose cause E4?
faq.a: Yes. Without level feedback the washer fills endlessly.
Check if water flows through an unplugged valve. If yes, replace valve.

title: Motor Error
shortDescription: The drive motor is not functioning properly.
fullDescription: E5 indicates the motor cannot start, stalls, or runs at wrong speeds.

fixSteps.title: Reduce load
fixSteps.instruction: IFB models have specific load capacities. Remove items to be within limit.

fixSteps.title: Check for jams
fixSteps.instruction: Rotate drum by hand. Listen for scraping.

fixSteps.title: Inspect brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Access motor. Replace if brushes shorter than 1 cm.

fixSteps.title: Test motor
fixSteps.instruction: Test windings and hall sensor with multimeter.

whenToCallPro: If motor or inverter needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E5 error motor fix

faq.a: Yes. Follow the kg capacity marked on your model.
Yes. Unplug immediately.
Some premium models use BLDC inverter motors without brushes.

title: Heating Error
shortDescription: Water heater not reaching target temperature.
fullDescription: E6 indicates the heater failed to reach the set temperature within expected time.

fixSteps.title: Test heater
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug. Access heater at bottom of tub. Test resistance (20-40 ohms). Check for ground fault.

fixSteps.title: Test NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test resistance (20K-50K ohms at room temp).

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect and reseat connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Replace heater or sensor. Ensure seal is seated.

whenToCallPro: If not comfortable with electrical components near water.
videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E6 error heater fix

faq.a: Yes. Many IFB cycles work well in cold water.
Yes. Can trip MCB and pose shock risk.
15-30 minutes depending on target temp.

title: Motor Speed Sensor Error
shortDescription: Motor speed sensor returning incorrect signals.
fullDescription: E7 indicates the control board cannot read motor speed accurately.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug 2 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect and reseat motor connections.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test resistance.

fixSteps.title: Inspect rotor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and check magnets.

videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E7 error speed sensor fix

faq.a: Usually yes, on the motor stator.
Can stall motor causing erratic readings.
Replace sensor first.

title: Water Level Sensor Fault
fullDescription: E8 means the water level sensor is stuck or out of range.

fixSteps.title: Check hose
fixSteps.instruction: Verify connected, not kinked.

fixSteps.title: Clear blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Blow through hose.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Blow into port, listen for click.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Swap and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If sensor fine but error persists.
videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E8 error water level sensor fix

title: Overflow Protection
fullDescription: E9 triggers when the base pan flood sensor detects water, indicating a leak.

fixSteps.title: Check for leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water, unplug, inspect underneath.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check for tears or debris.

fixSteps.title: Check internal hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten clamps, check for cracks.

fixSteps.title: Dry base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt, dry with towels, verify float.

whenToCallPro: If tub cracked or leak source unknown.
videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E9 error leak fix

title: Unbalanced Load Error
fullDescription: UE indicates the washer tried multiple redistribution cycles and cannot balance the load.

fixSteps.title: Redistribute
fixSteps.instruction: Open door, spread items evenly. IFB recommends mixing heavy and light items.

fixSteps.title: Level washer
fixSteps.instruction: Use spirit level. Adjust leveling feet.

fixSteps.title: Check transit bolts
fixSteps.instruction: On new installations, verify all transit bolts removed from rear.

fixSteps.title: Inspect suspension
fixSteps.instruction: Check shock absorbers if UE frequent.

videoSearchQuery: IFB washer UE error unbalanced fix

faq.a: Yes. Add similar weight items.
Very common on new IFB installations.
Yes, especially on tile floors common in India.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: Main and display boards cannot communicate.
fullDescription: E10 indicates communication failure between boards.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Reseat cable
fixSteps.instruction: Open top panel, reseat ribbon cable.

fixSteps.title: Clean connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Isopropyl alcohol on corroded pins.

fixSteps.title: Replace board
fixSteps.instruction: Display board first.

videoSearchQuery: IFB washer E10 error communication fix

faq.a: Yes. Use a voltage stabilizer, common practice in India.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
fullDescription: E11 indicates the NTC sensor is outside expected range.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug 2 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test resistance (20K-50K ohms room temp).

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Reseat connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Swap sensor.

whenToCallPro: If sensor fine but error persists.

title: Main Control Board Error
shortDescription: The main board has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription: F1 indicates the main control board has a critical fault from power surge, component failure, or voltage fluctuation, which is common in India.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug 10 minutes. Use a voltage stabilizer before plugging back in.

fixSteps.title: Check voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Verify outlet provides 220-240V. In India, voltage fluctuations are common.

fixSteps.title: Inspect board
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burned components or swollen capacitors.

fixSteps.title: Replace board
fixSteps.instruction: IFB service center recommended for board replacement.

whenToCallPro: IFB service center recommended for all board replacements.
videoSearchQuery: IFB washer F1 error control board fix

faq.a: In India, a voltage stabilizer is strongly recommended for all front-loading washers due to frequent voltage fluctuations.
If washer is less than 5 years old and otherwise good condition, yes.
Board replacement may be covered if within the warranty period. Check your warranty card.

title: Inverter Board Error
shortDescription: The motor inverter board has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: F2 indicates the inverter board controlling the BLDC motor has failed. Specific to IFB models with inverter drive.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Verify inverter board connectors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect board
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burned components.

fixSteps.title: Replace board
fixSteps.instruction: Order correct inverter board and swap.

whenToCallPro: IFB service center recommended for inverter board work.
videoSearchQuery: IFB washer F2 error inverter board fix
